query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in Chicago, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
For a commercial customer in Chicago (served by **Commonwealth Edison Co. / ComEd**, EIA ID 4110), customers with demand above 500 kW fall into ComEd's **Large Load** and **Very Large Load** delivery classes (ComEd's "Medium Load" tops out around 400 kW, so a >500 kW customer is into the Large Load class and above). Below are the demand charges from the OpenEI IURDB (current tariffs effective 1/1/2025, sourced via the OpenEI Utility Rate Database for that Chicago address).

## Demand charges for >500 kW commercial classes (ComEd)

| Service Class | Demand (capacity) charge | Effective |
|---|---|---|
| **RDS – Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** | **$14.58 / kW-month** | 2025 |
| **BES – Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** | **$14.58 / kW-month** | 2025 |
| **RDS – Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** | **$13.10 / kW-month** | 2025 |
| **BES – Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** | **$13.10 / kW-month** | 2025 |

(RDS = Retail Delivery Service; BES = "Basic" / Delivery with Standard Offer supply. The demand charge — the "flatdemandstructure" rate in the data — is identical between RDS and BES for a given class because it is the delivery/distribution demand charge; the two differ only in their energy rates.)

### At least two classes, clearly stated:
1. **Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** — demand charge **$14.58 per kW per month** (fixed monthly charge $183.89; energy ~$0.00124/kWh delivery on RDS).
2. **Very Large Load Delivery Class (Secondary)** — demand charge **$13.10 per kW per month** (fixed monthly charge $154.13; energy ~$0.00124/kWh delivery on RDS).

### Notes and assumptions
- I assumed the Chicago address (200 W Madison St, Chicago, IL 60606) to resolve the serving utility, which is **Commonwealth Edison (ComEd)**.
- These are "flat" (non-time-of-use) demand charges applied to the customer's monthly maximum demand in kW.
- A customer >500 kW would most likely be billed under the **Large Load** class (ComEd's Large Load class covers roughly 400 kW–10 MW), moving to **Very Large Load** at the very top end (≥10 MW). I included both so you have the relevant brackets.
- "Secondary" reflects service taken at secondary voltage (typical for most non-substation commercial customers); ComEd also has Primary-voltage variants of the Medium Load class (e.g., $8.90/kW for Medium Load Primary) at lower demand-charge rates for higher-voltage delivery.

**Source:** OpenEI Utility Rate Database (IURDB), retrieved via the `get_utility_tariffs` API for Commonwealth Edison Co. (EIA ID 4110), Commercial sector, currently active/approved tariffs effective January 1, 2025.
